parameters.jsCode: return [{ json: {"job_id": "PHY-30052466", "topic": "Millikan oil drop experiment", "title": "Millikan's Oil Drop: Unveiling Electron's Charge", "hashtags": "#physics #science #shorts #learnphysics", "narration": "How small is the fundamental unit of electric charge? Millikan's oil drop experiment precisely measured this elusive value. Microscopic oil droplets, negatively charged by friction, are sprayed into a chamber between two parallel plates. Initially, they fall under gravity, their terminal velocity revealing their mass. Then, an electric field is applied, causing the charged drops to rise or hover. By meticulously balancing gravitational and electric forces, Millikan calculated the charge on each drop. He discovered all charges were integer multiples of a single, fundamental unit: the electron's charge, 1.602 x 10^-19 Coulombs.", "clip_prompts": ["Cinematic slow-motion 9:16 vertical physics "Cinematic slow-motion 9:16 vertical physics simulation, several negatively charged oil droplets (glowing cyan spheres) falling at a constant terminal velocity of approximately 0.1 mm/s between two
